title: eriogonum effusum subsp. simpsonii
intro: Eriogonum effusum subsp. simpsonii, also known as Simpson's buckwheat, is a plant species within the buckwheat family (Polygonaceae). It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of this plant include reducing fever, soothing skin irritations, and supporting the immune system. Eriogonum effusum subsp. simpsonii can be prepared in various herbal forms, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are typically made from the plant's leaves, stems, or roots.
